# Splitting init functions is rejected: moving can change package
# initialization order, and copying duplicates init (Go allows multiple
# init funcs, so the package compiles but runs init twice).

! exec sflit -source big.go -sink small.go -regex '^init$' -move
stderr 'cannot move or copy init function'
cmp big.go expected_big.go
! exists small.go

# Copy mode (no -move) is rejected too.
! exec sflit -source big.go -sink copy.go -regex '^init$'
stderr 'cannot move or copy init function'
cmp big.go expected_big.go
! exists copy.go

-- big.go --
package p

var Seen []string

func init() {
	Seen = append(Seen, "big")
}

func Keep() {}
-- expected_big.go --
package p

var Seen []string

func init() {
	Seen = append(Seen, "big")
}

func Keep() {}
